categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ أَصْحَابِ الْكَهْفِ
categories.nameEn: Words from the story of the People of the Cave

items.verse: أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا عَجَبًا
items.verseEn: Or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder?

items.verse: إِذْ أَوَى ٱلْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةًۭ وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًۭا
items.verseEn: [Mention] when the youths retreated to the cave and said, "Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance."

items.verse: وَتَحْسَبُهُمْ أَيْقَاظًۭا وَهُمْ رُقُودٌۭ ۚ وَنُقَلِّبُهُمْ ذَاتَ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَذَاتَ ٱلشِّمَالِ ۖ وَكَلْبُهُم بَـٰسِطٌۭ ذِرَاعَيْهِ بِٱلْوَصِيدِ ۚ لَوِ ٱطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِرَارًۭا وَلَمُلِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ رُعْبًۭا
items.verseEn: And you would think them awake, while they were asleep. And We turned them to the right and to the left, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance. If you had looked at them, you would have turned from them in flight and been filled by them with terror.

items.verse: وَكَذَٰلِكَ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِيَتَسَآءَلُوا۟ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ قَآئِلٌۭ مِّنْهُمْ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍۢ ۚ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثْتُمْ فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ أَحَدَكُم بِوَرِقِكُمْ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ فَلْيَنظُرْ أَيُّهَآ أَزْكَىٰ طَعَامًۭا فَلْيَأْتِكُم بِرِزْقٍۢ مِّنْهُ وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ وَلَا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَدًا
items.verseEn: And similarly, We awakened them that they might question one another. Said a speaker from among them, "How long have you remained [here]?" They said, "We have remained a day or part of a day." They said, "Your Lord is most knowing of how long you remained. So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look to which is the best of food and bring you provision from it and let him be cautious. And let no one be aware of you.

categories.name: أَشْيَاءُ وَمَفَاهِيمُ مِنْ قِصَّةِ النَّبِيِّ سُلَيْمَان
categories.nameEn: Items and concepts from the story of Prophet Solomon

items.verse: قَالَ عِفْرِيتٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ أَنَا۠ ءَاتِيكَ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن تَقُومَ مِن مَّقَامِكَ ۖ وَإِنِّى عَلَيْهِ لَقَوِىٌّ أَمِينٌۭ
items.verseEn: A powerful one from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy."

items.verse: يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُۥ مَا يَشَآءُ مِن مَّحَـٰرِيبَ وَتَمَـٰثِيلَ وَجِفَانٍۢ كَٱلْجَوَابِ وَقُدُورٍۢ رَّاسِيَـٰتٍ ۚ ٱعْمَلُوٓا۟ ءَالَ دَاوُۥدَ شُكْرًۭا ۚ وَقَلِيلٌۭ مِّنْ عِبَادِىَ ٱلشَّكُورُ
items.verseEn: They made for him what he willed of elevated chambers, statues, bowls like reservoirs, and stationary kettles. [We said], "Work, O family of David, in gratitude." And few of My servants are grateful.

items.verse: إِذْ عُرِضَ عَلَيْهِ بِٱلْعَشِىِّ ٱلصَّـٰفِنَـٰتُ ٱلْجِيَادُ
items.verseEn: [Mention] when there were exhibited before him in the afternoon the poised [standing] racehorses.

items.verse: وَوَرِثَ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ دَاوُۥدَ ۖ وَقَالَ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ عُلِّمْنَا مَنطِقَ ٱلطَّيْرِ وَأُوتِينَا مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْمُبِينُ
items.verseEn: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ وَرَدَتْ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Words that appear only once in the Quran (hapax legomena)

items.verseEn: In both of them are two springs, spouting.

items.verse: وَرَبُّكَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ذُو ٱلرَّحْمَةِ ۖ لَوْ يُؤَاخِذُهُم بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ لَعَجَّلَ لَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۚ بَل لَّهُم مَّوْعِدٌۭ لَّن يَجِدُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَوْئِلًۭا
items.verseEn: And your Lord is the Forgiving, the possessor of mercy. If He were to impose blame upon them for what they earned, He would have hastened for them the punishment. Rather, for them is an appointment from which they will never find an escape.
[And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,

items.verse: قَالُوٓا۟ أَضْغَـٰثُ أَحْلَـٰمٍۢ ۖ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِتَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحْلَـٰمِ بِعَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: They said, "[It is but] a mixture of false dreams, and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams."
And [with] Pharaoh, owner of the stakes?
And We created you in pairs.

items.verse: وَإِذَا بَلَغَ ٱلْأَطْفَـٰلُ مِنكُمُ ٱلْحُلُمَ فَلْيَسْتَـْٔذِنُوا۟ كَمَا ٱسْتَـْٔذَنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And when the children among you reach puberty, let them ask permission [at all times] as those before them have done. Thus does Allāh make clear to you His verses; and Allāh is Knowing and Wise.

harf.hint: A seat of power for a king or queen.
harf.arabicVerse: قَالَ نَكِّرُوا۟ لَهَا عَرْشَهَا نَنظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِىٓ أَمْ تَكُونُ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ
harf.verse: 27:41 — He said, "Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided."

deduction.title: The First Covenant
deduction.intro: A story of honor, a test of obedience, and a moment of forgetfulness that changed the course of existence. This figure was given a lofty station and placed in a realm of peace, but a single prohibition stood between them and eternal bliss. A whisper of temptation led to a choice with consequences that would echo through every generation to come. Who is this soul?
clues: I was taught the names of all things, a knowledge that set me apart.
A great assembly was commanded to prostrate before me as a sign of respect, but one refused out of arrogance.
My spouse and I were given a beautiful garden to dwell in, with the freedom to enjoy its bounties.
We were warned against a single tree, but a cunning whisper convinced us that it held the key to immortality.
After we transgressed, our state of grace was removed, and we felt the shame of our nakedness.
I received words of repentance directly from my Lord, who in His mercy forgave me and established me as the first of my kind on Earth.

trial.label: What Was My Primary Test?
options: Splitting the sea with a staff
A test of immense patience through illness

location.label: What Was the Source of My Downfall?
options: A dispute over a garden's harvest

outcome.label: What Was the Consequence?
options: Being swallowed by a great fish
Receiving command over the wind and jinn
Descent to Earth to begin humanity's story
Being raised to a high place in the heavens
outcome.answer: Descent to Earth to begin humanity's story

deduction.verse: So the angels prostrated - all of them entirely, (15:30)

scramble.hint: A divine command to convey a message about two of Allah's most hopeful attributes.
scramble.arabic: ۞ نَبِّئْ عِبَادِىٓ أَنِّىٓ أَنَا ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
scramble.verseEn: [O Muḥammad], inform My servants that it is I who am the Forgiving, the Merciful,

verse.arabic_text: وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍۢ فَخُورٍۢ
verse.translation: And do not turn your cheek [in contempt] toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly. Indeed, Allah does not like everyone self-deluded and boastful.

theme_question.correct: The importance of humility and avoiding arrogance
options: The importance of humility and avoiding arrogance
The obligation to give charity to the poor
The prohibition of speaking ill of others
The command to honor one's parents

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is part of a series of profound counsels that Luqman the Wise gives to his son. These timeless pieces of advice, recorded in the Quran, cover core tenets of faith (Tawhid), character (akhlaq), and social conduct. This specific advice directly addresses the inner spiritual disease of arrogance and its outward manifestation in one's posture and behavior towards others.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is the condemnation of arrogance (`kibr`) and the command to practice humility. 'Turning your cheek' (`tuṣaʿʿir khaddaka`) is an idiom for showing contempt and disdain. Walking 'exultantly' (`maraḥan`) describes a boastful, proud gait. Allah reminds us that He dislikes those who are 'self-deluded and boastful' (`mukhtāl fakhūr`), connecting internal pride with external swagger. This verse teaches that true dignity lies not in looking down on others, but in humility before Allah and His creation.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Luqman is a Meccan surah named after the wise man Luqman, whose story and advice to his son form a central part of the surah. It emphasizes the wisdom behind Allah's creation, the oneness of Allah (Tawhid), the importance of honoring parents, and the establishment of good character. The surah contrasts the path of the wise and grateful with that of the ignorant and arrogant, using the counsels of Luqman as a practical guide for believers.
